mission.title: rook: Add support for dynamically resizing volumes provisioned with rook
mission.description: Add support for dynamically resizing volumes provisioned with rook. Requested by 45+ users.

steps.title: Check current rook deployment
steps.description:
Verify your rook version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
helm list -n rook 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working rook installation.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
Kubernetes 1.8 added the ability to resize persistent volume claims (https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#persistentvolumeclaimresize).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add support for dynamically resizing volumes provisioned…
steps.description:
This could be possible to implement in the volume provisioner, and may already have some logic in the upstream provisioner controller in Kubernetes external storage repo: https://github.com/kubernetes-incubator/external-storage/blob/master/lib/controller/controller.go

According to the sig-storage planning sheet, volume expansion will go to beta in k8s 1.11: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1t4z5DYKjX2ZDlkTpCnp18icRAQqOE85C1T1r2gqJVck/edit#gid=1308589045

We should also remember that

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add support for dynamically resizing volumes provisioned…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This could be possible to implement in the volume provisioner, and may already have some logic in the upstream provisioner controller in Kubernetes external storage repo: https://github.com/kubernetes-incubator/external-storage/blob/master/lib/controller/controller.go

According to the sig-storage planning sheet, volume expansion will go to beta in k8s 1.11:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
